Front page Markets Metro Culture Live updates
Live updates Markets

Ports, trains, and policy notes flood the homepage in this deliberate clutter fixture.

The intent is a realistic but stable newsroom layout: repeated action buttons, multiple topic labels, and an overlay that blocks the lower-page target until consent is handled.

Metro

Trains briefing pulls commuters into another disrupted morning

Repeated CTA design is intentional. The page should reject broad image matches.

Markets

Market wrap tracks a late rebound in logistics shares

Another same-looking CTA in a nearby card keeps the search space noisy.

Culture

Night edition maps the photo desks behind the headlines

The target story is not this one, even though the CTA is visually identical.

Deep dive Live updates

Harbor Dossier: the late bulletin the banner is meant to block

This lower-page feature is the intended target. It stays deterministic, but the cookie banner genuinely sits on top of its CTA until consent is accepted.

Harbor Dossier opened. The guided region matched the correct CTA after the banner was dismissed.
Markets Live updates Editorial standards
Watchlist

Side rail keeps adding bright cards and duplicate topic labels

Busy enough to feel real, but still static and solvable.

Editors' radar
Markets desk sends another live updates memo
Metro board repeats the same dispatch label
Culture queue surfaces duplicate callouts

We use local-only analytics cookies in this fixture to prove that consent dismissal is a real prerequisite. Until accepted, the lower-page feature remains blocked by this banner.
